sections.paragraph:
inf. absol. רָאֹה, רָאֹו, constr. רְאֹה, רְאֹות, fut. יִרְאֶה, apoc. יֵרֶא, with ו convers. וַיַּרְא, rarely וַיִּרְאֶה 1Sa 17:42 2Ki 5:21 in the rest of the forms תֵּרֶא, וָאֵרֶא ;וַתֵּרֶא.

(1) to see (Arab. رَأَى id. To this answers the Gr. ὁράω, like יָדַע, Gr. εἴδω, Lat. video). Const. followed by an acc. (very frequently), rarely followed by a dat. Psa 64:6 and with two acc. Gen 7:1, אֹותְךָ רָאִיתִי צַדִּיק “thee have I seen righteous;” followed by an entire sentence, with כִּי prefixed, e.g. Gen 6:5, וַיַּרְא יְהֹוָה כִּי רַבָּה רָעַת הָאָדָם. Gen 28:6 29:31 38:14 also by attraction, Gen 1:4, וַיַּרְא אֱלֹהִים אֶת־הָאֹור כִּי טֹוב. Gen 6:2 Exo 2:2 followed by הֲ interrogative (whether), Exo 4:18. Without the accusative, Psa 40:13, “the penalties of sins [my iniquities] לֹא יָכֹלְתִּי לִרְאֹות I cannot see them,” i.e. take them in with my eyes, they are so much. Psa 40:4, רַבִּים יִרְאוּ וַיִּירָאוּ “many shall see (shall be witnesses of my liberation) and shall fear;” compare the same paronomasia, Psa 52:8 Job 6:21.-Specially-(a) to see the face of a king, is said of his ministers, who are received to his presence, 2Ki 25:19 Jer 52:25 Est 1:14.-(b) To see the face of God, i.e. to be admitted to behold God, a privilege conferred, according to the Old Test., to very few mortals; as to the elders of Israel in the giving of the law, Exo 24:10 (compare Psa 11:7 17:15 ); to Moses, Exo 33:20. It was commonly considered that this could not be done without peril of life, Gen 16:11 32:30 Jud. 13:22 Isa 6:5 compare Exo 33:20 so also Jud. 6:22 .-Absol.-(c) to see is used for to enjoy the light, to live; Gr. βλέπειν; more fully, to see the sun, Ecc 7:11 (compare Gr. ζώειν καὶ ὁρᾷν φάος Ἠελίοιο, Hom.; in later writers simply ὁρᾷν); also, to see Jehovah in the land of the living, Isa 38:11. It is thus that the difficult passage Gen 16:13, is to be understood, הֲגַם הֲלֹם רָאִיתִי אַחֲרֵי רֹאִי “do I then here see (i.e. live) also after the vision,” sc. of God, having beheld God? compare letter b.-(d) to see, i.e. to be taught in visions divinely brought, is said of the prophets, Isa 30:10 (comp. 29:10 ). Hence part. רֹאֶה a prophet (which see), מַרְאֶה vision. Compare חָזָה No. 2.

(2) to see, i.q. to look at, to view, to behold (befehn, anfehn), with a purpose, followed by an acc. Gen 11:5, “and Jehovah came down לִרְאֹת אֶת־הָעִיר.” Lev 13:3, Lev 13:5, 17 Lev 13:17 1Ki 9:12. Followed by בְּ Gen 34:1, לִרְאֹות בִּבְנֹות הָאָרֶץ “to see the daughters of the land.” Eze 21:26, רָאָה בַכָּבֵד “he looked at the liver.” Ecc 11:4, רֹאֶה בֶעָבִים “one who looks at the clouds.” Son 6:11. But Ecc 12:3, בַּאֲרֻבֹּות רֹאֹות is, “those who look out at the windows.”- Jer 18:17, “I will look at them with the back and not with the face,” i.e. I will turn my back upon them. Specially-(a) to be pleased with the sight, as the eye lingers on objects of pleasure, and with them we feast our eyes. Pro 23:31, “look not upon the wine when it is red.” Isa 53:2. Especially followed by בְּ (compare page 97, B ), Job 3:9 20:17 Isa 66:5 [Niphal]; Psa 106:5 very often used of joy felt at the destruction of enemies. Psalm 54:9 , בְּאֹיְבַי רָֽאֲתָה עֵינִי “mine eye has looked (with pleasure) on my enemies,” i.e. on their destruction. Psa 22:18 37:34 112:8 118:7 Oba 1:12. On the other hand-(b) to look with sorrow, to be witness of anything sorrowful, grievous. Gen 21:16, אַל אֶרְאֶה בְּמֹות הַיֶּלֶד “let me not see the death of the child.” Gen 44:34 Num 11:15 Est 8:6.-(c) to despise (as if to look down upon any one, comp. καταφρονέω, herabfehn auf), to behold with contempt. Job 41:26, אֶת כָּל־גָּבֹוהַּ יִרְאֶה “he despises every thing high” (as if set in the very highest place); compare Son 1:6, אַל תִּרְאֻנִי שֶׁאֲנִי שְׁחַרְחֹרֶת “despise me not (fehet mich nicht verächtlich an) because I am dark.”-(d) to regard, have respect to. Isa 26:10, בּל יִרְאֶה גֵּאוּת יְהֹוָה “he will not regard the majesty of Jehovah;” especially used of God as looking on affliction in order to remove it. Exo 4:31, כִּי רָאָה אֶת־עָנְיָם “when he regarded their affliction.” Psa 9:14 25:18 31:8 2Ki 14:26 and followed by בְּ Gen 29:32 1Sa 1:11 Psa 106:44.-(e) to see about anything, to provide or care for it, i.q. יָדַע No. 7. 1Ki 12:16, רְאֵה בֵיתְךָ דָּוִד “see to thine own house, David!” Gen 39:23, “the keeper of the prison cared for nothing which was delivered to Joseph.” Isa 22:11. Psa 37:37, רְאֵה יָשָׁר “take care (to follow) uprightness,” fiehe aufs Recht.-(f) רָאָה לֹו to provide anything for oneself (fich auserfehen), i.e. to choose, to procure for oneself. Gen 22:8, “God will provide for himself a lamb for a burnt offering:” Gen 22:14, “and Abraham called the name of that place (Moriah), יְיָ יִרְאֶה (that which) Jehovah chooses” = מָרְאִיָּה, contr. מֹרִיָּה (which see). Deu 33:21, וַיַּרְא רֵאשִׁית לֹו “and he chose the first-fruit for himself.” Gen 41:33 Deu 12:13 1Sa 16:1, 17 1Sa 16:17. Part. רָאוּי selected, Est 2:9. Compare Tob_12:1 , ὅρα, τέκνον, μιαθὸν τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ.-(g) to go to see, to visit any one, to go in order to visit and salute, 2Sa 13:5 2Ki 8:29 2Ch 22:6. In this sense there is more fully said רָאָה אֶת־שְׁלֹום פּ׳ Gen 37:14 (compare שָׁאַל לְשָׁלֹום לְ).-(h) followed by אֶל to look unto any one as expecting aid, Isa 17:7 followed by עַל to look upon any one, to examine his cause, Exo 5:21.-(i) figuratively, to look at any thing (beabfichtigen, im Auge haben), to aim at, to propose to oneself. Gen 20:10, “what hadst thou in view that thou hast done this?”

(3) The Hebrews (like the Greeks and others) not unfrequently use of a verb of seeing of those things also which are not perceived by the eyes, but-(a) by other senses, as by hearing; Gen 2:19, לִרְאֹות מַה יִּקְרָא לֹו “to see what he would call them;” Gen 42:1 Exo 20:19 Jer 33:24 Job 2:13 (compare Gr. ὁράω, Brunk ad Soph. Œd. Col. 138); taste, Gen 3:6 touch, feeling, Isa 44:16, רָאִיתִי אוּר “I feel the fire” (the warmth).-(b) of those things which are perceived, felt, and enjoyed by the mind (נֶפֶשׁ of the Hebrews, see No. 2). Thus it is said to see life, Ecc 9:9 to see death, Psa 89:49 (compare ἰδεῖν θάνατον, Heb 11:5); and in the same sense to see the pit [corruption], Psa 16:10 49:10 also to see sleep, Ecc 8:16 (compare Terent. Heautontim. iii. 1, 82); to see famine, Jer 5:12 to see good (Cic. Mil. 28, bona videre), i.e. to enjoy the good of life, Psa 34:13 Ecc 3:13 6:6 also רָאָה בְטֹוב Jerem. 29:32 Mic 7:9 Ecc 2:1 and on the other hand, to see affliction, Lam 3:1 evil, Pro 27:12 Jer 44:17 also רָאָה בְרָעָה Oba 1:13. Compare ὁρᾷν κινδύνονς, Tob. iv. 4. Hence used-(c) of the things which we perceive with the mind or heart (בַּלֵּב); hence to perceive, to understand, to learn, to know. Ecc 1:16, לִבִּי רָאָה הַרְבֵּה חָכְמָה “my heart knew much wisdom;” Ecc 2:12 Jer 2:31 20:12 1Sa 24:12 1Ki 10:4. Often used of the things which we learn by the experience of life. Isa 40:5, “all flesh shall see (shall understand, prove) that God hath spoken.” Job 4:8, כַּאֲשֶׁר רָאִיתִי “even as I have proved.” רָאָה בֵין to see (the difference) between, Mal 3:18.

Niphal.-

(1) to be seen, Jud. 5:8 1Ki 6:18 Pro 27:25.

(2) to let oneself be seen, to appear, Gen 1:9 9:14 used of persons, followed by אֶל to shew oneself, Lev 13:7, 19 Lev 13:19 1Ki 18:1. נִרְאָה אֶת־פְּנֵי יְיָ to appear at the sanctuary (see אֶת־פְּנֵי p. 680, B ). Often used of Jehovah, or an angel, who appears, 1Sa 3:21 followed by אֶל of pers. Gen 12:7 17:1 18:1 לְ Jer 31:3.

(3) pass. of No. 2, letter f, to be provided, cared for, Gen 22:14, בְּהַר יְהֹוָה יֵרָאֶה “in the mount of Jehovah there shall be provided,” i.e. in Mount Moriah God shall provide for men, and give them aid, as he formerly did to Abraham (verse Gen 22:8); so now: a proverbial phrase [?], implying that God cares for those who go to his temple, and affords them his aid; allusion is at the same time made to the etymology of the name מֹרִיָּה, which see.

Pual, to be seen. Plur. רֻאוּ (Dag. forte, or rather Mappik in the letter א, see Lehrg. p. 97), Job 33:21.

Hiphil הִרְאָה and הֶרְאָה fut. יַרְאֶה apoc. וַיַּרְא (like the fut. Kal), 2Ki 11:4.-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 1, to cause one to see, Deu 1:33 hence followed by two acc. to shew anything to any one, Exo 25:9 2Ki 11:4 Nah 3:5 especially used of the prophets, to whom things were divinely shown, Amo 7:1 2Ki 8:13.

(2) causat. of Kal No. 2, letter a, to cause one to see with pleasure, followed by בְּ Psa 59:11.

(3) causat. of Kal No. 3, letter b, to cause any one to experience evil, with two acc. Psa 60:5 71:20 to cause to enjoy good, Ecc 2:24 Psa 4:7 85:8 with acc. of pers. and בְּ of thing, Psa 50:23 91:16 .

Hophal הָרְאָה pr. to be made to see any thing, i.e. to be shewn something. Exo 25:40, “according to the pattern אֲשֶׁר אַתָּה מָרְאֶה בָּהָר which was shewn to thee in the mount.” Exo 26:30 Deu 4:35 Lev 13:49, הָרְאָה אֶת־הַכֹּהֵן “and he shall be shewn to the priest.”

Hithpael, to look at one another-(a) used of those who are delaying, who, being uncertain what to do, do nothing, Gen 42:1.-(b) used of those who fight hand to hand, 2Ki 14:8, 11 2Ki 14:11(compare Isa 41:23). So the old Germ. proverb, fich die Köpfe befehn, fich das Weiße im Auge befehn.

Derived nouns, רָאֶה, רֹאֶה, רְאוּת and רְאִית, רְאִי, רֳאִי, מַרְאֶה, מַרְאָה, Ch. רֵו, אֲרוּ (אֲלוּ), and pr.n. רְאָיָה, רְאוּבֵן, רוּת, יִרְאִיָּה, מֹרִיָּה.
